context: Tests whether the agent implements the Strategy + Factory + Null Object calculator pattern: a frozen SERVICE_MAP dispatch table in the Factory, a NullService that returns safely for all unknown variants, the prescribed file structure, the single-call public API, and concrete services that override the correct methods.

checklist.name: SERVICE_MAP constant
checklist.description: The Factory class defines a SERVICE_MAP constant (frozen hash) that maps variant keys to service classes — not a case statement or if/elsif chain

checklist.name: Factory NullService fallback
checklist.description: The Factory's .for method returns a NullService instance (or NullService.new) when the variant is not found in SERVICE_MAP or when the qualifying context is nil/inactive

checklist.name: NullService never raises
checklist.description: The NullService class does NOT raise any exception under any input — its calculate (or equivalent public) method returns nil or a safe value for all inputs

checklist.name: Correct file structure
checklist.description: The module contains at least factory.rb, base_service.rb, null_service.rb, and one concrete service file — all under app/services/<module_name>/

checklist.name: Single entry point API
checklist.description: The client-facing usage demonstrated (in code, specs, or documentation) is Factory.for(entity).calculate — a single chained method call, not a multi-step instantiation

checklist.name: Concrete overrides should_calculate?
checklist.description: At least one concrete service class overrides a should_calculate? (or equivalent guard) method that adds a variant-specific check on top of calling super

checklist.name: Concrete overrides compute_result
checklist.description: At least one concrete service class overrides compute_result (or equivalent calculation) method containing the variant-specific business logic

checklist.name: NullService spec context
checklist.description: The spec file contains a context or describe block that explicitly tests the NullService path — e.g. unknown variant, nil entity, or inactive qualifier returns the null object

checklist.name: Variant spec coverage
checklist.description: The spec file includes at least one example per concrete variant (e.g. standard tier, express tier) verifying the factory dispatches to the correct service class

checklist.name: Frozen string literal
checklist.description: Every Ruby file in the module begins with # frozen_string_literal: true

checklist.name: SERVICE_MAP key type consistency
checklist.description: The SERVICE_MAP keys are the same type (all strings or all symbols) as what would be retrieved from the entity attribute — not a mix that would cause silent NullService fallbacks
